completion:
I will first identify strong privacy-preserving learning baselines and their main performance bottlenecks, then propose a method that combines complementary techniques rather than relying on one privacy mechanism alone.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"privacy-preserving data mining differential privacy federated learning secure aggregation predictive performance"}
```

[result]: Recent work commonly combines federated learning, differential privacy, and secure aggregation. A recurring limitation is that strong privacy noise often reduces model accuracy, especially for sparse users, minority classes, or heterogeneous client data.

To improve prediction while preserving privacy, I will focus on adaptive privacy budgeting, representation sharing, and privacy-safe knowledge transfer.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"adaptive differential privacy federated learning knowledge distillation heterogeneous data accuracy privacy tradeoff"}
```

[result]: Relevant studies suggest that adaptive clipping, client-level privacy accounting, and federated knowledge distillation can reduce the accuracy loss caused by differential privacy. However, many approaches still leak information through gradients, use fixed noise schedules, or perform poorly under non-IID user data.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"A privacy-preserving data mining method that combines federated learning, secure aggregation, adaptive differential privacy, and privacy-safe knowledge distillation. Each user trains locally; encrypted model updates are aggregated through secure aggregation; differential privacy noise is allocated adaptively according to gradient sensitivity, client contribution stability, and class imbalance. A public or synthetically generated proxy dataset is then used for federated knowledge distillation so the global model can learn smoother decision boundaries without exposing raw user records or individual logits.","niche_genome":"The method targets privacy-sensitive predictive mining tasks such as healthcare risk prediction, financial fraud detection, mobile behavior modeling, and recommendation systems where user data are distributed, non-IID, and legally restricted from central collection.","observation_genome":"Standard differential privacy improves privacy but often harms accuracy because the same clipping and noise schedule is applied to all clients, features, and training phases. Federated learning avoids raw-data sharing but can still leak information through gradients. Secure aggregation hides individual updates but does not by itself control memorization or inference attacks. Combining these methods with adaptive privacy control and distillation can preserve privacy while recovering predictive performance.","limitation_genome":"The method requires reliable client participation, cryptographic aggregation infrastructure, and careful privacy accounting. If the proxy or synthetic distillation data are low quality, the accuracy gain may be limited. Very small populations or highly unique user patterns may still require stronger noise, reducing performance.","delta_genome":"Compared with conventional privacy-preserving data mining, the key improvement is dynamic privacy allocation: clients, classes, and training rounds that pose higher leakage risk receive stronger protection, while low-risk stable updates receive less destructive noise. Distillation on privacy-safe proxy data further improves generalization without exposing raw data or individual gradients.","claim_genome":"This method can provide formal privacy protection while improving model prediction performance over fixed-noise differentially private federated learning, especially under heterogeneous user data, by reducing unnecessary noise and transferring aggregate knowledge through privacy-safe distillation."}}
```

topic: [v31/zh-en/computer_science] 研究数据库查询优化中的代价估计误差问题，并提出一种基于机器学习的自适应优化方法。

Cost estimation errors in query optimization often arise from independence assumptions, stale statistics, and poor cardinality estimates under correlated predicates. I will first search for representative work on learned cardinality/cost estimation and adaptive query optimization.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"database query optimization cost estimation errors machine learning adaptive learned cardinality estimation"}
```

[result]: Recent work highlights that cardinality estimation is the dominant source of query optimizer cost errors. Learned estimators such as MSCN, NeuroCard, and tree/graph-based models improve estimates, while adaptive query processing uses runtime feedback to correct plans during or after execution.

To ground the proposal, I will read a representative source on learned cardinality estimation and feedback-driven optimization.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"learned cardinality estimation query optimizer runtime feedback adaptive reoptimization"}
```

[result]: The literature suggests two complementary directions: offline learned models trained on query workloads and database samples, and online feedback loops that update estimates using observed cardinalities and latencies. Key limitations include workload drift, uncertainty calibration, optimizer integration overhead, and lack of robust fallback mechanisms.

Based on these findings, I propose a hybrid adaptive optimizer that combines learned estimation, uncertainty-aware planning, and execution feedback.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Train a machine learning cost-estimation layer that predicts cardinalities and operator costs from query-plan features, table statistics, predicate encodings, join graphs, and lightweight data samples. At optimization time, combine the learned prediction with the traditional optimizer estimate using uncertainty-aware weighting; during execution, collect actual cardinalities and latencies to update the model or trigger re-optimization when errors exceed a threshold.","niche_genome":"The method targets analytical and mixed workloads where traditional cost models fail because of correlated predicates, skewed distributions, complex joins, user-defined functions, or stale statistics. It is especially useful for systems that repeatedly execute related query templates and can benefit from workload-specific learning.","observation_genome":"A practical design should not replace the database optimizer entirely. Instead, it should act as an adaptive correction module: predicting where the native optimizer is likely wrong, calibrating cost estimates, and using runtime feedback to improve future decisions. Confidence estimation is crucial because learned models can be worse than hand-engineered models under workload shift.","limitation_genome":"The approach requires representative training data, incurs feature-extraction and inference overhead, and may overfit to historical workloads. Online updates must be carefully controlled to avoid instability, and integration with commercial optimizers may be limited by restricted access to internal cost-model components.","delta_genome":"Compared with static learned cardinality estimators, the proposed method adds uncertainty-aware fusion with the native optimizer and runtime feedback-driven adaptation. Compared with classic adaptive query processing, it uses learned generalization across queries rather than only correcting individual executions.","claim_genome":"A hybrid machine-learning-based adaptive optimizer can reduce cost-estimation error and improve plan quality by learning workload-specific correction patterns while preserving the robustness of traditional optimizers through uncertainty calibration, feedback loops, and conservative fallback policies."}}
```

```
[result]: Existing approaches include trace-based ranking, service dependency graphs, random walk over anomalous call graphs, graph neural networks, and causal Bayesian or Granger-style inference. Limitations include noisy telemetry, changing topology, weak labels, difficulty distinguishing correlation from causation, and poor generalization across deployments.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Faults in cloud-native microservice architectures propagate through service call chains, retry amplification, circuit-breaker misconfiguration, queue backpressure, shared database/cache/network dependencies, resource starvation, and orchestration-level events such as pod eviction or autoscaling delay. The mechanism can be represented as a time-evolving heterogeneous dependency graph where nodes are services, pods, queues, databases, and hosts, and edges encode RPC, message, storage, and infrastructure dependencies with temporal latency, error-rate, saturation, and throughput signals.","niche_genome":"Design an automated anomaly detection and root cause localization method for dynamic cloud-native microservices using multi-modal observability data: metrics, logs, distributed traces, Kubernetes events, and service mesh telemetry. When an anomaly occurs, the system should extract the anomalous subgraph, estimate temporal-causal influence among services, and rank root-cause candidates by combining anomaly severity, propagation direction, dependency proximity, and causal precedence.","limitation_genome":"Major limitations are telemetry noise, missing traces, high-cardinality metrics, cold-start services, topology drift after deployment, and the risk of mistaking downstream symptoms for upstream causes. Label scarcity also makes purely supervised models fragile, while purely correlation-based methods can over-rank popular gateway services or shared infrastructure components.","delta_genome":"The proposed improvement is a hybrid RCA pipeline: dynamic graph construction plus self-supervised anomaly detection plus causal propagation scoring. It differs from static thresholding or simple correlation by using temporal ordering, dependency-aware message passing, deployment-event awareness, and counterfactual suppression tests to distinguish true root causes from propagated symptoms.","claim_genome":"The resulting system can automatically detect microservice anomalies and localize likely root causes by identifying the earliest causally influential abnormal node in the dynamic dependency graph. It should reduce mean time to detection and diagnosis compared with manual dashboard inspection or threshold-only alerting, while remaining deployable in Kubernetes/service-mesh environments with limited labeled incident data."}}
```

[result]: Recent empirical studies on China’s Green Credit Guidelines and Green Finance Reform and Innovation Pilot Zones find that green finance policies increase firms’ environmental investment and green innovation, mainly by easing financing constraints for compliant firms and raising financing costs for high-pollution firms. Several papers report heterogeneous effects by ownership, with state-owned enterprises often responding more strongly due to policy alignment and easier access to green credit, while private firms face stronger financial frictions.

To refine the comparison, I read evidence on mechanisms and ownership differences in enterprise responses to green financial regulation.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"ownership heterogeneity green credit policy environmental investment SOE private enterprises financing constraints carbon emission reduction investment"}
```

[result]: The literature suggests three main mechanisms: preferential credit allocation, reputational/political incentives, and regulatory pressure. SOEs tend to have closer bank-government ties and stronger political incentives to comply, while private firms may be more sensitive to financing costs but less able to obtain green funding unless they have strong disclosure, collateral, or green certification.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Green finance policies incentivize corporate carbon-reduction investment by lowering the cost of capital for low-carbon projects, redirecting credit away from carbon-intensive activities, improving the market value of credible environmental disclosure, and increasing regulatory and reputational pressure on firms to decarbonize.","niche_genome":"The most relevant setting is China’s bank-dominated financial system, where green credit guidelines, green bonds, and green finance pilot zones shape firms’ access to external finance. The ownership comparison is especially salient because state-owned enterprises and private enterprises differ in political connections, financing constraints, and policy responsiveness.","observation_genome":"State-owned enterprises are expected to show a stronger and faster investment response because they face direct policy mandates, have easier access to bank credit, and can use carbon-reduction investment to satisfy government performance objectives. Private enterprises may also increase green investment, but their response is more conditional on whether green finance actually relaxes financing constraints, improves market reputation, or provides measurable cost advantages.","limitation_genome":"A key empirical challenge is separating the effect of green finance policy from simultaneous environmental regulation, industrial upgrading, local subsidies, and carbon-market pilots. Another limitation is that reported green investment may include symbolic or compliance-oriented spending rather than genuine emissions-reduction capital expenditure.","delta_genome":"Compared with a generic policy-effect analysis, the contribution is to emphasize ownership-based heterogeneity: green finance is not a uniform incentive, because SOEs react more through political and credit-allocation channels, whereas private firms react more through financing-cost and market-signaling channels.","claim_genome":"Green finance policy generally promotes corporate carbon-reduction investment, but the incentive effect is stronger and more immediate for state-owned enterprises than for private enterprises. topic: [v31/zh-en/economics_finance] 分析金融科技发展对普惠金融水平的提升作用，并研究其在城乡居民金融服务可得性中的差异。
full_prompt: 分析金融科技发展对普惠金融水平的提升作用，并研究其在城乡居民金融服务可得性中的差异。
completion:
Fintech likely improves inclusive finance through digital payment, mobile banking, online credit scoring, and lower transaction costs, but its effects may differ because rural residents face weaker infrastructure and lower digital literacy.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"fintech development inclusive finance urban rural financial service accessibility China digital finance empirical evidence"}
```

[result]: Studies on China’s digital inclusive finance show that fintech expands financial access by reducing geographic distance, lowering service costs, and enabling alternative credit assessment. Evidence also suggests heterogeneous effects: urban residents benefit more from richer digital ecosystems, while rural gains depend strongly on mobile internet coverage, agent networks, and digital skills.

To refine the mechanism and inequality channel, I would read empirical work using digital inclusive finance indices, household survey data, and urban-rural heterogeneity tests.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"empirical mechanisms fintech inclusive finance access urban rural households digital divide"}
```

[result]: The literature commonly identifies three mechanisms: payment convenience, credit availability through data-based risk assessment, and broader wealth-management/insurance access. However, the urban-rural gap persists because rural households often have lower smartphone usage quality, weaker financial literacy, thinner credit histories, and fewer complementary offline institutions.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Fintech raises inclusive finance by lowering transaction costs, weakening geographic constraints, expanding digital payments, and using alternative data to improve credit screening for underserved households and small businesses.","niche_genome":"The effect is especially relevant in economies with large urban-rural financial service gaps, where traditional bank branches are concentrated in cities and rural clients face high search, travel, and information costs.","observation_genome":"Urban residents usually experience stronger immediate gains in financial service accessibility because they have better internet infrastructure, higher digital literacy, denser merchant acceptance networks, and more complete credit records. Rural residents also benefit, particularly through mobile payments and online microcredit, but the improvement is more conditional on digital infrastructure and education.","limitation_genome":"Fintech may not automatically equalize access because the digital divide, algorithmic exclusion, data privacy risks, over-borrowing, and weak rural financial literacy can limit or even distort its inclusive effects.","delta_genome":"Compared with traditional inclusive-finance policies focused on physical bank expansion, fintech shifts the key constraint from branch distance to digital capability, data availability, platform governance, and last-mile connectivity.","claim_genome":"Fintech significantly improves the overall level of inclusive finance, but its impact on financial service accessibility is uneven: it narrows some rural access barriers while simultaneously creating new urban-rural differences based on digital infrastructure, literacy, and platform participation."}}
```

```action

```

[result]: The literature commonly finds that tighter LTV, DTI, countercyclical capital buffers, and mortgage risk-weight rules can reduce credit-fueled housing booms. Effects are strongest when household leverage is high and mortgage credit is a dominant funding channel.

Next, I will read focused evidence on transmission mechanisms and heterogeneity across borrower balance sheets and housing market cycles. This supports a sharper causal claim.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"studies on LTV DTI macroprudential tightening household leverage housing price cycles"}
```

[result]: Empirical studies suggest macroprudential tightening dampens housing price growth mainly by restricting marginal borrowers’ mortgage access and slowing leverage accumulation. However, policy effects may weaken when credit shifts to shadow banking, when supply constraints dominate prices, or when expectations of future appreciation remain strong.

Now I will synthesize the research design into a structured hypothesis genome suitable for an economics-finance study. The proposal should specify mechanism, empirical niche, observable outcomes, limitations, contribution, and claim.

```action
{"tool":"propose","context":"macroprudential policy effects on real estate price volatility with credit constraints and household leverage"}
```

[result]: A credible design can combine regional housing price volatility, macroprudential policy intensity, household debt-to-income ratios, and mortgage credit constraints. Identification may rely on policy timing, regional leverage heterogeneity, and interaction terms between policy tightening and household leverage.

Final propose:

The main transmission channel is that constrained households reduce speculative and leveraged housing demand, thereby weakening credit-driven price amplification.",

topic: [v31/zh-en/computer_science] 研究量子计算对经典密码算法安全性的潜在影响，并讨论后量子密码算法的设计与实现挑战。
full_prompt: 研究量子计算对经典密码算法安全性的潜在影响，并讨论后量子密码算法的设计与实现挑战。
completion:
Quantum threats split sharply between public-key and symmetric cryptography, so I first check the core algorithmic mechanisms and security impact.  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"quantum computing impact on RSA ECC AES SHA Shor Grover post-quantum cryptography overview"}
```
[result]: Shor’s algorithm can break RSA, Diffie–Hellman, and elliptic-curve cryptography by solving integer factorization and discrete logarithms efficiently on a sufficiently large fault-tolerant quantum computer. Grover’s algorithm gives a quadratic speedup against brute-force search, weakening symmetric keys and hash preimage resistance but not destroying them if key and digest sizes are increased.

Next I examine practical post-quantum cryptography standardization and deployment issues, since the challenge is not only mathematical security but also implementation in real systems.  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"NIST post-quantum cryptography standards Kyber Dilithium implementation challenges side-channel performance migration"}
```
[result]: NIST has selected lattice-based schemes such as ML-KEM/Kyber for key establishment and ML-DSA/Dilithium for signatures, with hash-based signatures also used for conservative security. Reported challenges include larger keys/signatures, bandwidth and latency costs, side-channel resistance, constant-time implementation, cryptographic agility, interoperability, and migration of legacy protocols and hardware.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Quantum computing threatens classical public-key cryptography primarily through Shor’s algorithm, which can efficiently solve the factoring and discrete-logarithm problems underlying RSA, finite-field Diffie–Hellman, DSA, and elliptic-curve cryptography. Grover’s algorithm gives only a quadratic advantage against symmetric encryption and hash preimage search, so AES and SHA-family constructions can often be adapted by increasing key sizes or output lengths.","niche_genome":"The most urgent security niche is long-lived confidentiality and infrastructure identity: encrypted data captured today may be decrypted later once large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computers exist, while certificates, software updates, VPNs, TLS, blockchains, and identity systems depend heavily on vulnerable public-key primitives. Post-quantum cryptography targets classical computers and networks but uses problems believed to resist both classical and quantum attacks, such as lattice, code, hash, multivariate, and isogeny-related assumptions.","observation_genome":"Current standardization favors lattice-based schemes such as ML-KEM/Kyber for key encapsulation and ML-DSA/Dilithium for digital signatures because they offer strong performance and practical key sizes compared with many alternatives. However, implementation is nontrivial: schemes can have larger public keys, ciphertexts, or signatures than ECC; require careful randomness; must be protected against timing, power, cache, and fault side-channel attacks; and must be integrated into TLS, PKI, embedded devices, HSMs, smart cards, and constrained IoT systems.","limitation_genome":"Post-quantum security assumptions are newer and less time-tested than RSA or ECC, and some candidate families have already suffered cryptanalytic breaks or parameter revisions. Migration also faces operational limits: legacy devices may lack memory or bandwidth, certificate chains may grow, hybrid deployments increase complexity, and interoperability failures can undermine security even when the underlying algorithm is sound.","delta_genome":"A prudent transition strategy is hybrid cryptography, combining classical ECC/RSA with post-quantum KEMs or signatures during migration, while building cryptographic agility so algorithms and parameters can be replaced without redesigning entire systems. Research and engineering should focus on side-channel-hardened implementations, formal verification, efficient hardware/software co-design, robust randomness generation, protocol-level testing, and long-term monitoring of quantum progress and cryptanalysis.","claim_genome":"Quantum computing will not make all classical cryptography obsolete, but it will fundamentally break today’s dominant public-key systems once scalable fault-tolerant machines become available. The central computer-science challenge is therefore to deploy post-quantum algorithms early enough to protect long-lived data while managing performance, implementation security, standardization, and migration risks."}}
```

A smaller stream links investor attention to ESG salience, suggesting that attention may transmit ESG information into market pricing and financing outcomes.

Next, I will refine the empirical logic by checking how investor attention is typically operationalized and why it can serve as a mediating channel.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"investor attention mediator ESG disclosure cost of capital empirical proxies Baidu index media coverage analyst attention"}
```

[result]: Investor attention is commonly proxied by search index data, media coverage, analyst following, or online discussion intensity. The mediation logic is plausible: high-quality ESG disclosure attracts investor attention, improves information processing and recognition, reduces uncertainty and adverse selection, and ultimately lowers firms’ financing costs.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Higher ESG disclosure quality lowers listed firms’ financing costs by reducing information asymmetry, improving perceived transparency, and decreasing investors’ required risk premium. topic: [v31/zh-en/economics_finance] 考察地方政府债务扩张对区域经济增长质量的影响，重点评估债务风险与公共投资效率之间的关系。
full_prompt: 考察地方政府债务扩张对区域经济增长质量的影响，重点评估债务风险与公共投资效率之间的关系。
completion:
I first scope evidence on how local government debt affects growth quality, separating short-run investment stimulus from longer-run risk and efficiency channels.  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"local government debt expansion regional economic growth quality debt risk public investment efficiency China empirical evidence"}
```
[result]: Studies commonly find that local government debt can support infrastructure-led growth in the short run, but excessive debt raises fiscal risk, crowds out private investment, and weakens total factor productivity. The effect is often nonlinear, with growth-quality benefits turning negative beyond a debt-risk threshold.

I then focus on mechanisms linking debt risk to public investment efficiency, especially financing platforms, soft budget constraints, and misallocation.  
```action
{"tool":"read","query":"local government financing vehicles debt risk public investment efficiency misallocation regional growth quality"}
```
[result]: The literature highlights that local government financing vehicles may increase infrastructure supply but also encourage low-return projects when repayment responsibility is unclear. Higher debt pressure can distort fiscal expenditure toward land finance, refinancing, and visible construction projects, reducing public capital efficiency and inclusive growth quality.

Based on this, I formulate a testable research claim with mechanism, niche, observation, limitation, delta, and claim genomes.  
```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Local government debt expansion affects regional economic growth quality through two competing channels: it relaxes infrastructure financing constraints and raises public capital formation, but rising debt risk increases soft-budget behavior, refinancing pressure, crowding-out of private investment, and misallocation toward low-return public projects.","niche_genome":"The study focuses on Chinese regional governments where infrastructure investment, land finance, and local government financing vehicles make the debt-growth relationship especially sensitive to public investment efficiency and fiscal risk thresholds.","observation_genome":"Moderate debt may improve growth quality by supporting transport, utilities, and public services, while excessive debt risk is expected to reduce total factor productivity, innovation capacity, private-sector vitality, and green or inclusive growth outcomes.","limitation_genome":"Identification is challenged by reverse causality between growth quality and borrowing capacity, measurement error in hidden debt, and regional heterogeneity in fiscal capacity, industrial structure, and investment governance.","delta_genome":"The contribution is to move beyond the simple debt-growth level effect and evaluate whether public investment efficiency mediates or moderates the impact of debt risk on high-quality regional development, including possible nonlinear threshold effects.","claim_genome":"Local government debt expansion has an inverted-U effect on regional economic growth quality: when debt risk is controlled and public investment efficiency is high, debt supports quality growth, but once debt risk exceeds a fiscal sustainability threshold, inefficient public investment becomes the main channel through which debt expansion harms growth quality."}}
```
[result]: The proposed framework yields a clear empirical strategy: test nonlinear debt-risk effects, estimate mediation through public investment efficiency, and compare effects across regions with different fiscal capacity and financing-platform dependence.

Final propose:
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Local government debt expansion affects regional economic growth quality through two competing channels: it relaxes infrastructure financing constraints and raises public capital formation, but rising debt risk increases soft-budget behavior, refinancing pressure, crowding-out of private investment, and misallocation toward low-return public projects.","niche_genome":"The study focuses on Chinese regional governments where infrastructure investment, land finance, and local government financing vehicles make the debt-growth relationship especially sensitive to public investment efficiency and fiscal risk thresholds.","observation_genome":"Moderate debt may improve growth quality by supporting transport, utilities, and public services, while excessive debt risk is expected to reduce total factor productivity, innovation capacity, private-sector vitality, and green or inclusive growth outcomes.","limitation_genome":"Identification is challenged by reverse causality between growth quality and borrowing capacity, measurement error in hidden debt, and regional heterogeneity in fiscal capacity, industrial structure, and investment governance.","delta_genome":"The contribution is to move beyond the simple debt-growth level effect and evaluate whether public investment efficiency mediates or moderates the impact of debt risk on high-quality regional development, including possible nonlinear threshold effects.","claim_genome":"Local government debt expansion has an inverted-U effect on regional economic growth quality: when debt risk is controlled and public investment efficiency is high, debt supports quality growth, but once debt risk exceeds a fiscal sustainability threshold, inefficient public investment becomes the main channel through which debt expansion harms growth quality."}}

Next, I will focus on the two policy shocks—Shanghai-Hong Kong Stock Connect in 2014 and Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect in 2016—and how they can be compared empirically.

A feasible design is to split the sample into pre- and post-launch windows, estimate volatility spillover networks among Shanghai, Shenzhen, Hong Kong, and possibly global control markets, and compare total, directional, and net spillover measures.

topic: [v31/zh-en/computer_science] 分析多模态学习中图像、文本与语音特征融合的关键挑战，并设计一种高效的跨模态表示学习框架。
full_prompt: 分析多模态学习中图像、文本与语音特征融合的关键挑战，并设计一种高效的跨模态表示学习框架。
completion:
I first scan for recent synthesis on multimodal fusion challenges to ground the framework in known failure modes such as modality imbalance, missing modalities, and representation misalignment.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"multimodal learning image text speech fusion challenges modality alignment missing modalities representation learning efficient cross-modal framework"}
```

[result]: Recent surveys emphasize three recurring bottlenecks: heterogeneous feature spaces, temporal/spatial alignment across modalities, and robustness under noisy or missing modalities. Efficient approaches increasingly combine contrastive pretraining, lightweight cross-attention, modality-specific encoders, and shared latent spaces.

I then read representative work on cross-modal representation learning to identify design patterns that scale beyond pairwise image-text learning to image-text-speech settings.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"cross-modal contrastive learning image text audio speech shared latent space fusion transformer"}
```

[result]: Strong frameworks typically use modality-specific front-end encoders, projection heads into a common embedding space, contrastive or matching losses for alignment, and attention-based fusion for task-specific reasoning. For efficiency, late or hybrid fusion, low-rank adapters, sparse cross-attention, and knowledge distillation reduce computation while retaining cross-modal interaction.

I synthesize these findings into a compact framework proposal that addresses alignment, complementarity, robustness, and computational cost.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Design a hybrid cross-modal representation framework with three modality-specific encoders for image, text, and speech, followed by lightweight projection layers into a shared semantic space. Use multi-granularity alignment: global contrastive learning for sample-level correspondence, token-frame-region matching for fine alignment, and sparse cross-attention for adaptive fusion. Add modality dropout and reconstruction heads to improve robustness when one modality is noisy or absent.","niche_genome":"The framework targets multimodal tasks where image, text, and speech coexist but differ in structure, sampling rate, noise level, and semantic density, such as video understanding, multimodal dialogue, assistive AI, educational agents, and human-computer interaction.","observation_genome":"Key challenges include semantic heterogeneity, temporal-spatial misalignment, modality dominance, redundant or conflicting signals, high computational cost, and incomplete modalities. Image features are spatial and dense, text is symbolic and discrete, while speech contains both linguistic and paralinguistic cues, making direct concatenation insufficient.","limitation_genome":"The proposed framework may still depend on large paired datasets and careful negative sampling. This reduces computation while improving robustness through contrastive alignment, modality dropout, and uncertainty-aware fusion weights.","claim_genome":"An efficient image-text-speech representation learner should combine modality-specific encoders, shared latent alignment, sparse cross-modal attention, and robustness objectives. This architecture can deliver better generalization, lower inference cost, and stronger performance under missing or noisy modalities than simple feature concatenation or fully dense multimodal fusion."}}
```

[result]: Proposal generated with a mechanism, target niche, observed challenges, limitations, novelty delta, and central claim.

At inference, a confidence-aware fusion module assigns dynamic weights to each modality and supports graceful degradation when one modality is missing.","niche_genome":"This framework is suitable for multimodal understanding tasks involving image, text, and speech, including video question answering, multimodal dialogue, embodied AI, accessibility systems, medical or educational assistants, and social media content analysis.","observation_genome":"The central challenge in multimodal fusion is not merely combining features, but resolving heterogeneity across modalities. Dynamic fusion also requires calibration, because overconfident weighting may suppress useful minority-modal signals.","delta_genome":"The main improvement over simple concatenation, early fusion, or dense full-attention fusion is the combination of shared-space contrastive alignment with efficient sparse cross-modal interaction. topic: [v31/zh-en/economics_finance] 分析人民币汇率波动对出口企业盈利能力的影响，重点关注汇率风险管理工具的使用效果。
full_prompt: 分析人民币汇率波动对出口企业盈利能力的影响，重点关注汇率风险管理工具的使用效果。
completion:
I will first locate empirical evidence linking RMB exchange-rate movements to Chinese exporters’ margins, then check findings on whether hedging instruments reduce profit volatility or improve performance.  
```action
{"tool":"search","query":"RMB exchange rate volatility export firms profitability hedging derivatives empirical evidence China exporters"}
```  
[result]: Studies generally find that RMB appreciation compresses exporters’ RMB-denominated revenues and gross margins, especially for low-margin, labor-intensive, and high import-substitution firms. Exchange-rate volatility also increases cash-flow uncertainty, but the effect varies with market power, imported-input share, foreign-currency debt, and pricing-to-market ability.

I will now read for more specific evidence on foreign-exchange risk management tools such as forwards, options, swaps, natural hedging, and currency invoicing.

I will first identify the main technical comparison points: quantum-enabled protein–ligand modeling, classical molecular dynamics, and efficiency metrics such as runtime, accuracy, sampling, and hardware readiness.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"quantum computing drug chemistry protein ligand interaction simulation compared with molecular dynamics efficiency"}
```

[result]: Recent literature frames quantum computing as promising for electronic-structure accuracy in drug discovery, especially for binding-site chemistry, polarization, charge transfer, and reaction-like interactions. However, classical molecular dynamics remains far more mature and efficient for large-scale conformational sampling on current hardware.

I will now focus on a more specific synthesis: where quantum methods could outperform MD and where they currently remain limited.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"hybrid quantum classical algorithms VQE quantum chemistry protein ligand binding free energy molecular dynamics limitations NISQ"}
```

[result]: Hybrid quantum-classical methods such as VQE may eventually improve high-accuracy quantum chemistry calculations for ligand binding and active-site energetics. Current NISQ devices are constrained by qubit count, noise, circuit depth, and state-preparation overhead, so practical protein–ligand simulations still rely mostly on classical MD, enhanced sampling, docking, QM/MM, and free-energy perturbation workflows.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Quantum computing can contribute to protein–ligand interaction simulation by solving difficult electronic-structure subproblems, such as polarization, charge transfer, metal coordination, protonation effects, and covalent or near-covalent binding chemistry, using hybrid quantum-classical algorithms like VQE or future fault-tolerant methods such as quantum phase estimation. Rather than replacing molecular dynamics, quantum methods are most plausible as high-accuracy modules embedded within classical MD, QM/MM, docking, or free-energy workflows.","niche_genome":"The most relevant interdisciplinary niche is quantum-assisted medicinal chemistry for binding-site energetics, where classical force fields may be inaccurate because they approximate electrons implicitly. This is especially valuable for metalloproteins, enzyme inhibitors, covalent drugs, highly polar ligands, and systems where small electronic effects strongly influence affinity or selectivity.","observation_genome":"Compared with traditional molecular dynamics, quantum computing is not currently more efficient for full protein–ligand simulation. Classical MD on GPUs can simulate large biomolecular systems and produce trajectories for conformational sampling, while present quantum devices are limited to small molecular fragments and short, noisy circuits. The likely near-term advantage of quantum approaches is not trajectory speed but improved local energetic accuracy for selected chemical regions.","limitation_genome":"The main limitations are NISQ hardware noise, insufficient logical qubits, expensive state preparation, measurement overhead, limited treatment of solvent and protein flexibility, and the difficulty of integrating quantum electronic calculations with long-timescale conformational sampling. Classical MD also has limitations, including force-field bias and poor sampling of rare events, but it is currently much more scalable and operationally efficient.","delta_genome":"The key research delta is to develop hybrid pipelines that use classical MD for broad conformational sampling and quantum algorithms for accurate rescoring or refinement of representative binding poses and transition states. Efficiency should be evaluated not only by wall-clock time, but by accuracy per computational cost, reduction in false positives, improved prediction of binding free energy, and usefulness in lead optimization.","claim_genome":"Quantum computing is best viewed as a complementary accelerator for chemically difficult subproblems in protein–ligand modeling, not as a near-term replacement for molecular dynamics. Traditional MD remains more efficient for large-scale sampling today, while quantum-enhanced methods may become superior for high-precision binding energetics once fault-tolerant hardware and robust hybrid workflows mature."}}
```

```action

The system should include human-in-the-loop validation so that computational outputs remain compatible with philological standards.","niche_genome":"The framework targets ancient and premodern textual corpora, especially classical Chinese texts with multiple transmitted editions, variant characters, unstable segmentation, historical place names, personal names, official titles, reign periods, bibliographic references, and uncertain textual provenance. ```action

```

[result]: Recent studies commonly use MPC as a safety- or stability-oriented low-level controller, while DRL adapts reference trajectories, cost weights, terminal constraints, or obstacle-avoidance behaviors. Reported challenges include MPC computational burden, DRL policy safety guarantees, and degraded tracking when obstacle avoidance becomes aggressive.

To sharpen the research gap, I will examine whether existing hybrid MPC-DRL methods provide formal stability discussion while maintaining real-time operation in dynamic obstacle scenarios.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"hybrid MPC deep reinforcement learning mobile robot tracking stability constraints dynamic obstacles real-time control"}
```

[result]: The literature suggests that many hybrid approaches improve avoidance and adaptability, but stability is often empirical rather than formally guaranteed. Methods with stronger safety constraints tend to increase computation time, while lightweight DRL policies improve real-time response but may weaken constraint satisfaction and tracking robustness.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Develop a hierarchical hybrid controller in which MPC performs constrained local path tracking with stability-oriented terminal costs and safety constraints, while a DRL module adaptively tunes MPC weights, prediction horizon, and obstacle-risk penalties according to environmental uncertainty and robot state.","niche_genome":"Dynamic and uncertain mobile robot navigation scenarios where classical MPC alone is too conservative or computationally expensive, and pure DRL lacks reliable stability and safety guarantees.","observation_genome":"Existing MPC-DRL path-tracking methods often improve adaptability and obstacle avoidance, but they rarely analyze the three-way trade-off among closed-loop stability, real-time computational feasibility, and dynamic obstacle-avoidance performance in a unified framework.","limitation_genome":"The approach may still depend on the quality of DRL training scenarios, and formal stability guarantees can become conservative when the learned adaptation changes MPC parameters online.","delta_genome":"Compared with standard MPC or end-to-end DRL, the proposed method explicitly separates safety-critical control from learned adaptation and evaluates how adaptive MPC parameterization shifts the balance between tracking accuracy, computation time, and collision avoidance success.","claim_genome":"A stability-aware MPC controller augmented by DRL-based online parameter adaptation can achieve better path-tracking robustness and obstacle-avoidance performance in dynamic uncertain environments while preserving real-time feasibility more effectively than either standalone MPC or standalone DRL."}}

I will first gather standard robust/adaptive control patterns for robot manipulators, then shape them into a concise research design with validation metrics.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"adaptive robust control multi-joint robot manipulator payload variation friction disturbance modeling uncertainty trajectory tracking"}
```

[result]: Common approaches combine computed-torque or sliding-mode structures with adaptive estimation of uncertain dynamics. Friction is often represented by viscous/Coulomb/Stribeck terms, while payload variation is handled through online parameter adaptation or disturbance observers.

Next, I will refine the design around a practical implementation that can be validated in simulation and hardware.

```action

[result]: Effective experimental controllers usually include: a nominal inverse-dynamics model, adaptive laws for uncertain inertial/load parameters, robust damping or saturation-based sliding terms, and disturbance estimation for unmodeled friction. [result]: Recent work emphasizes integral/terminal sliding surfaces, adaptive gain tuning, disturbance observers, and boundary-layer or high-order sliding modes to handle wheel slip, slope disturbances, and model uncertainty while reducing chattering.

I then focus on the trade-off between fast finite-time convergence and actuator-exciting discontinuous control, because this is central for wet or rough terrain.

[result]: Terminal or nonsingular fast terminal sliding modes improve convergence speed, but larger switching gains and sharper nonlinear exponents increase chattering. Saturation, tanh/sigmoid switching, super-twisting, and adaptive gains reduce chattering, though excessive smoothing can slow convergence or leave steady-state tracking errors.

```action

Early inflammatory mediators including IL-1β, TNF-α, IL-6, C1q, C3, and reactive oxygen species are linked to synaptic dysfunction and complement-mediated synapse pruning.

```

[result]: The literature suggests a dual role: early microglial activation can compact amyloid plaques and clear debris, but chronic or dysregulated activation promotes inflammasome signaling, cytokine release, complement tagging of synapses, and loss of synaptic integrity. TREM2-dependent disease-associated microglia may initially restrict plaque toxicity, but impaired or excessive activation can worsen neuritic dystrophy and synapse elimination.

Adult visual cortex plasticity is well studied through paradigms such as monocular deprivation, environmental enrichment, and altered visual statistics, but the key gap is linking experience-dependent functional changes to specific circuit rewiring in adulthood.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"adult visual cortex plasticity sensory experience change monocular deprivation optogenetics single unit recordings inhibitory circuits"}
```

[result]: Studies show that adult visual cortex retains limited but measurable plasticity after altered visual experience, especially when inhibition, neuromodulation, or behavioral state is manipulated. Monocular deprivation and altered visual environments can shift ocular dominance, receptive field tuning, and excitatory-inhibitory balance.

To design a mechanistic experiment, the study should combine longitudinal activity measurements with causal perturbation of candidate circuit elements such as parvalbumin interneurons, somatostatin interneurons, or long-range feedback inputs.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"optogenetic manipulation parvalbumin interneurons adult V1 plasticity ocular dominance circuit reorganization"}
```

[result]: Prior work suggests that inhibitory interneuron circuits gate adult cortical plasticity, with parvalbumin interneurons regulating critical-period-like constraints and somatostatin interneurons influencing dendritic integration. Optogenetic suppression or activation during altered visual experience can test whether these interneuron classes are necessary or sufficient for adult circuit remodeling.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Altered adult visual experience induces reorganization of excitatory-inhibitory microcircuits in primary visual cortex, including changes in ocular dominance, receptive field tuning, and interneuron-mediated gain control. The central mechanism to test is whether reduced or redistributed inhibition from parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons permits adult excitatory neurons to retune after sensory perturbation.","niche_genome":"The study focuses on adult mouse primary visual cortex after sensory experience change, using monocular deprivation or exposure to altered visual statistics such as orientation-enriched environments. This niche addresses adult cortical plasticity rather than developmental critical-period plasticity.","observation_genome":"Record single-unit or two-photon calcium responses from identified V1 neurons before, during, and after 7-14 days of altered visual experience. Measure ocular dominance index, orientation selectivity, receptive field structure, trial-to-trial reliability, and correlated activity among excitatory neurons and interneuron subclasses.","limitation_genome":"A major limitation is that functional retuning does not by itself prove anatomical circuit rewiring. Optogenetic manipulation may also produce nonphysiological firing patterns, and monocular deprivation may not generalize to all forms of adult sensory experience change.","delta_genome":"The study adds causal circuit validation by combining longitudinal single-cell recording with cell-type-specific optogenetics. Complementary optogenetic tagging can identify interneuron types during recording.","claim_genome":"If altered adult visual experience changes tuning and ocular dominance in excitatory neurons, and these changes are bidirectionally controlled by optogenetic manipulation of inhibitory interneurons, then adult visual cortical plasticity depends on experience-driven rebalancing of local inhibitory circuits rather than only passive sensory adaptation."}}

A suitable starting point is to verify which chronic stress paradigms reliably alter medial prefrontal cortex function while preserving compatibility with repeated imaging and behavioral testing.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"chronic stress mouse model medial prefrontal cortex calcium imaging behavior neurotransmitter dopamine glutamate GABA"}
```

[result]: Chronic unpredictable mild stress, chronic restraint stress, and social defeat stress are widely used to model stress-related prefrontal dysfunction. Studies commonly report altered mPFC excitability, impaired cognitive flexibility, anxiety/depression-like behavior, and changes in glutamate, GABA, dopamine, and serotonin signaling.

Next, I would read representative methods combining in vivo calcium imaging with behavioral and neurochemical assays to identify a feasible integrated design.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"fiber photometry or miniscope calcium imaging medial prefrontal cortex chronic stress mice behavioral assays neurotransmitter measurement"}
```

[result]: Fiber photometry is suitable for longitudinal population-level calcium recording in defined mPFC neurons during behavior, while miniscopes provide cellular resolution. Neurotransmitters can be measured using microdialysis-HPLC, fast-scan cyclic voltammetry, or genetically encoded sensors such as GRAB-DA, iGluSnFR, and GABA sensors.

Final propose:

```action

Express GCaMP in mPFC pyramidal neurons, and optionally in PV or SST interneurons, to monitor stress-induced changes in neural activity during behavior.","niche_genome":"The model is designed for systems neuroscience analysis of chronic stress effects on prefrontal circuits, linking longitudinal calcium imaging, behavioral phenotyping, and neurotransmitter quantification in the same experimental framework.","observation_genome":"Compare control and stressed mice across sucrose preference, open field, elevated plus maze, forced swim or tail suspension, and attentional set-shifting or working-memory tasks. Record mPFC calcium dynamics during baseline, stress exposure, and task performance, then measure extracellular dopamine, glutamate, and GABA using microdialysis-HPLC or genetically encoded neurotransmitter sensors.","limitation_genome":"Chronic stress paradigms can show variability across sex, strain, housing condition, and stress intensity. Fiber photometry lacks single-cell resolution, while miniscope imaging is more invasive; neurotransmitter measurements also differ in temporal resolution depending on the method used.","delta_genome":"The key advance is an integrated longitudinal model that connects stress history, real-time mPFC circuit activity, behavioral deficits, and neurochemical remodeling rather than measuring these endpoints separately.","claim_genome":"This animal model can reveal how chronic stress remodels prefrontal cortical circuit dynamics and neurotransmitter balance, thereby identifying circuit-level mechanisms underlying stress-induced cognitive and affective dysfunction."}}
```

completion:
Astrocytic potassium buffering during seizures is mechanistically centered on Kir4.1 channels, Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase activity, and astrocyte gap-junction coupling, so I would first verify the main measurable variables used in this field.

```action
{"tool":"search","query":"astrocytes potassium homeostasis seizures Kir4.1 Na K ATPase gap junction extracellular potassium electrophysiology calcium imaging"}
```

[result]: Recent literature links impaired astrocytic K⁺ clearance to seizure susceptibility, especially through Kir4.1 dysfunction, reduced Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase-dependent uptake, and altered connexin-mediated spatial buffering. Common readouts include extracellular K⁺ concentration, seizure threshold/duration, local field potentials, astrocyte membrane potential, and astrocytic Ca²⁺ activity.

To sharpen the question, I would focus on a causal perturbation that can be experimentally tested during induced epileptiform activity.

```action
{"tool":"read","query":"key physiological indicators astrocyte potassium buffering epileptiform activity extracellular K seizure local field potential Kir4.1"}
```

[result]: Experiments often combine acute brain slices or in vivo seizure models with K⁺-sensitive electrodes, patch-clamp recordings, LFP/EEG, calcium imaging, and pharmacological or genetic manipulation of Kir4.1, Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase, or connexins. A measurable hypothesis is that impaired astrocytic K⁺ buffering increases extracellular K⁺ accumulation, neuronal hyperexcitability, and seizure burden.

```action
{"tool":"propose","gene_genome":{"mechanism_genome":"Astrocytes regulate extracellular potassium during seizures through Kir4.1-mediated K+ uptake, Na+/K+-ATPase-dependent clearance, and connexin-based spatial buffering across astrocytic networks.","niche_genome":"The research question targets astrocyte-neuron ionic interactions in epileptic cortical or hippocampal circuits during seizure initiation and propagation.","observation_genome":"Key detectable physiological indicators include extracellular K+ concentration dynamics, seizure threshold, seizure duration and frequency, EEG/LFP power and spike burden, neuronal firing rate, astrocyte membrane potential, astrocytic Ca2+ transients, Kir4.1 current density, Na+/K+-ATPase activity, and connexin-mediated dye or electrical coupling.","limitation_genome":"A major limitation is that extracellular K+ changes are both a cause and consequence of neuronal firing, making it difficult to separate primary astrocytic failure from secondary seizure-driven ionic disturbance.","delta_genome":"The proposed study would compare normal and selectively impaired astrocytic K+ buffering during controlled epileptiform stimulation to determine whether astrocyte-specific disruption is sufficient to lower seizure threshold and prolong epileptiform activity.","claim_genome":"A concise research question is: Does impairment of astrocyte-mediated potassium buffering promote seizure initiation and propagation by increasing extracellular K+ accumulation and neuronal hyperexcitability? This can be tested by manipulating astrocytic Kir4.1, Na+/K+-ATPase, or connexin coupling while measuring extracellular K+, EEG/LFP epileptiform activity, neuronal firing, and astrocyte physiological responses."}}
```

Altered ratios of excitatory to inhibitory markers in prefrontal cortex, hippocampus, and striatum have been associated with impaired sociability in ASD models.

```action

```

[result]: Recent studies associate post-TBI BBB leakage with microglial activation, astrocytosis, peripheral immune-cell infiltration, and later cognitive impairment. Common readouts include contrast-enhanced MRI, Evans blue or fluorescent dextran leakage, IgG extravasation, Iba1/GFAP immunostaining, cytokine profiling, and Morris water maze or novel object recognition.

I will next read targeted sources on mechanisms and experimental designs that connect spatial BBB permeability to inflammatory propagation.

```action

```

[result]: Evidence suggests BBB breakdown can permit serum proteins, danger signals, and leukocytes to enter perilesional and remote brain regions, amplifying microglial and astrocytic activation. Longitudinal imaging can map barrier leakage over time, while immunostaining can test whether inflammatory markers expand beyond the initial injury core and whether this expansion predicts cognitive deficits.

```action
